pages.text: Fuel items are inserted into the network to fill the $(thing)Furnace Fuel$(), $(thing)Blaze Fuel$(), and $(thing)Bottle Fuel$() pools. Shift-click fuel items from your inventory in any terminal to convert them.

$(thing)Furnace Fuel values:$()$(br)$(li)$(item:minecraft:coal) Coal: $(thing)1,600$() fuel$(li)$(item:minecraft:charcoal) Charcoal: $(thing)1,600$() fuel$(li)$(item:minecraft:coal_block) Coal Block: $(thing)16,000$() fuel$(li)$(item:minecraft:lava_bucket) Lava Bucket: $(thing)20,000$() fuel

$(thing)Blaze Fuel values:$()$(br)$(li)$(item:minecraft:blaze_rod) Blaze Rod: $(thing)2,400$() furnace + $(thing)1,200$() blaze$(li)$(item:minecraft:blaze_powder) Blaze Powder: $(thing)600$() blaze$(br2)A Blaze Rod gives $(thing)both$() furnace and blaze fuel simultaneously!

$(thing)Bottle Fuel:$()$(br)$(li)Glass Bottle or any Potion: $(thing)1$() bottle fuel per item.$(br2)$(thing)Multi-pool items:$() When an item can fuel multiple pools, you'll see a selection popup to choose which pool to fill. For example, Blaze Rods can go into either Furnace Fuel or Blaze Fuel.
